title: Spectrumite
body:
Hello there! Welcome to the Demo of my new mod; Spectrumite! (Mod was influenced by AI)

This mod is of low priority and will rarely recieve updates.

This mod includes (currently made, but there is a lot more planned) :

 

Primary Materials

🔥 1. Crimsonite (Red)

Hue: Deep, shimmering crimson with iridescent highlights.

Strength: 1,800 MPa (stronger than tungsten).

Properties:

Heat-resistant to ~5,000°C.

Corrosion-proof and magnetically neutral.

Used in spacecraft, armor, and luxury items.

Lore: Formed in the cores of dying stars, making it exceptionally rare and valuable.

 

🟠 2. Emberium (Orange)

Hue: Glowing ember-orange with faint golden flecks, giving it a molten appearance.

Strength: ~1,600 MPa (similar to Inconel).

Properties:

Self-repairing lattice structure—minor scratches or dents heal over time.

Emits low-level heat, making it warm to the touch.

Resistant to electrical surges, perfect for power conduits.

Lore: Said to be forged from the molten heart of volcanoes on a distant, volcanic exoplanet.

 

🟡 3. Aurorium (Yellow)

Hue: Brilliant, sunlit gold with a faint, shimmering aura.

Strength: ~2,200 MPa (stronger than Crimsonite, making it nearly indestructible).

Properties:

Reflects and amplifies light, making it glow faintly in darkness.

Naturally radiates warmth without losing mass.

Extremely conductive, used in advanced energy grids.

Lore: Legend claims that Aurorium was once the armor of ancient celestial beings, making it a symbol of divine power.

 

🟢 4. Viridite (Green)

Hue: Vivid, emerald green with a glass-like translucency.

Strength: ~1,200 MPa (similar to steel, but much lighter).

Properties:

Flexible yet incredibly strong—can bend without breaking.

Bio-reactive: It bonds with organic material, making it ideal for medical implants or armor with regenerative properties.

Self-cleaning surface, immune to dirt or grime.

Lore: Found deep within alien rainforests, Viridite is believed to be part of an ancient, living ecosystem of sentient plants.

 

🔵 5. Azuriteon (Blue)

Hue: Vivid cobalt blue with a subtle, swirling pattern that resembles waves.

Strength: ~1,500 MPa (stronger than steel, lighter than titanium).

Properties:

Water-repellent and hydrophobic, making it perfect for aquatic applications.

Can channel and store kinetic energy, releasing it in controlled bursts.

Remains cool under extreme heat.

Lore: Known as the "Ocean's Metal," Azuriteon is said to be forged from the cores of mythical deep-sea leviathans.

 

🟣 6. Amethyrium (Purple)

Hue: Rich, royal purple with a shifting, opalescent sheen.

Strength: ~2,000 MPa (similar to titanium alloys, but more flexible).

Properties:

Naturally absorbs and dissipates harmful radiation.

Can temporarily phase-shift, becoming semi-ethereal (intangible) under the right frequency of light.

Used in stealth technology and radiation shielding.

Lore: Amethyrium is rumored to come from asteroids near neutron stars, absorbing cosmic radiation during its formation.

 

⚫ 7. Obscurite (Black) – Bonus Shade

Hue: Deep, light-absorbing black with a faint, star-like sparkle.

Strength: ~3,000 MPa (the strongest of the rainbow metals).

Properties:

Completely absorbs light, rendering it nearly invisible in the dark.

Nullifies sound waves, making it perfect for stealth technology.

Resistant to all forms of energy-based attacks.

Lore: Obscurite is believed to be mined from the remnants of collapsed black holes, making it one of the rarest and most dangerous materials in existence.

 

🌈 Rainbow Alloy: Spectrumite

If you want the ultimate fusion, imagine an alloy combining all seven metals—Spectrumite.

Hue: Prismatic, shifting colors that subtly change with the light.

Strength: ~2,500 MPa (nearly indestructible).

Properties:

Adapts to its environment—heatproof, waterproof, and corrosion-proof.

Emits a low-level glow of multicolored light.

Used exclusively in legendary weapons, royal regalia, or futuristic space tech.

Lore: Said to be the crystallized essence of a supernova, Spectrumite is nearly impossible to forge, requiring immense energy and mythical craftsmanship.

 

Secondary Materials

💜 1. Laventium (Lavender Purple)

Hue: Soft lavender with a shimmering, pearlescent finish that gives off a faint, magical glow.

Strength: ~1,900 MPa (stronger than steel, slightly less than Crimsonite).

Properties:

Memory Metal: It can "remember" its shape and reform after being bent or damaged.

Ethereal Glow: Emits a soothing, lavender-hued bioluminescent light in darkness.

Sound Dampening: Naturally reduces vibrations, making it perfect for stealth technology or noise-canceling armor.

Lore: Laventium is said to be harvested from the petals of cosmic flowers blooming on the edges of reality, making it a symbol of peace and serenity.

 

🌥️ 2. Ceruleal (Sky Blue)

Hue: A soft, sky-blue metal with a faint iridescent shimmer that resembles sunlit clouds.

Strength: ~2,100 MPa (stronger than titanium, lighter than steel).

Properties:

Buoyant Density: Incredibly lightweight—so light it can float in water, making it ideal for aerial and aquatic vehicles.

Electrical Repulsion: Repels and disperses static electricity and ionized particles.

Cool Touch: Always remains slightly cool to the touch, regardless of the surrounding temperature.

Lore: Said to be found in floating islands suspended in the upper atmosphere of gas giants, Ceruleal is considered a sacred material used in skyships and celestial temples.

 

🌌 3. Voidium (Space Black)

Hue: Deep, light-absorbing black with a subtle, swirling nebula-like shimmer when viewed at certain angles.

Strength: ~3,500 MPa (the strongest of all the metals so far—borderline indestructible).

Properties:

Light Absorption: Completely absorbs 99.9% of light, making it appear as a void in physical form.

Gravity-Warping: Slightly alters gravitational fields around it, reducing its own weight by 10% in low gravity.

Energy Sink: Nullifies energy-based attacks by dispersing them into its core.

Lore: Formed from the compressed remnants of collapsed stars, Voidium is so rare that it’s believed only one small asteroid of it exists in the known universe. Those who possess it are rumored to become unstoppable.

 

⚪ 4. Luminaeon (Bright White)

Hue: Blindingly bright, pure white with a crystalline luster that creates rainbow-like refractions in direct light.

Strength: ~2,400 MPa (nearly as strong as Voidium, but much lighter).

Properties:

Self-Luminous: Constantly emits a soft, ambient glow, making it visible in total darkness.

Purity Field: Repels toxins, radiation, and corruption, making it perfect for medical and purification technology.

Light Infusion: Can store and release bursts of pure light energy, blinding or illuminating its surroundings.

Lore: Believed to be crystallized starlight, Luminaeon is said to have been used by celestial guardians to forge weapons capable of cutting through darkness itself.

 

🌌✨ Bonus: Nebularium (Lavender + Sky Blue + Space Black + Bright White Alloy)

If you want the ultimate fusion, imagine an alloy combining these four metals: Nebularium.

Hue: Shifting colors that transition smoothly from lavender to sky blue with veins of glowing white, all on a backdrop of swirling black void.

Strength: ~3,800 MPa (the strongest known fictional metal).

Properties:

Gravity and Light Control: Can absorb or reflect light and gravity, creating localized light or dark zones.

Phased Density: Becomes temporarily intangible, passing through solid objects before re-materializing.

Energy Infusion: Channels pure cosmic energy, making it a power source for mythical relics.

Lore: Nebularium is believed to be the crystallized essence of a nebula's birth and death, holding both the creation and destruction of stars within its core.

 

Tertiary Materials (Runic Abilities!!!)

🌿 1. Verdantine (Chartreuse – Yellow-Green)

Hue: A luminous chartreuse with subtle golden highlights, giving it a bioluminescent glow in low light.

Strength: ~1,400 MPa (similar to high-grade steel but far more flexible).

Properties:

Living Metal: Infused with bio-reactive energy, Verdantine can self-regenerate minor damage over time.

Nature Bond: Reacts to living organisms, strengthening when worn or wielded by sentient beings.

Photosynthetic Energy: Absorbs light to gradually repair and fortify itself.

Lore:
Found only on the lush, forested planet Sylvaris, Verdantine is believed to be the crystallized lifeblood of the ancient, sentient trees that once ruled the world. Warriors of old wore Verdantine armor that would mend itself, making them nearly immortal in battle.
